stock_or_policy_anchor: 172.2 mb U.S. IEA allocation; DOE had released 17.5 mb by week ending 2026-04-24
duration_basis: allocation divided by F6R low/base/high inventory-draw flow
assumptions: Uses IEA allocation as available emergency bridge, not as confirmed permanent net supply. Actual physical release by late April was much smaller at 17.5 mb.
replenishment_limits: SPR release is structured as an exchange; drawability depends on DOE schedule, refinery/logistics fit, and later return barrels.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Direct U.S. Hormuz volume is small relative to total consumption; global price exposure is larger than direct volume exposure.

buffer_metric: Approximate EU share of IEA release
stock_or_policy_anchor: 80 mb approximate EU contribution to IEA release
duration_basis: approximate release volume divided by F6R low/base/high inventory-draw flow
assumptions: Uses European Commission statement that EU countries contribute about 20% of the 400 mb IEA action.
replenishment_limits: EU release should be paired with demand restraint; product bottlenecks, especially jet fuel, can bind before crude stock availability.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: EU aggregate direct crude exposure is moderate; Mediterranean product stress may be higher than aggregate row implies.

stock_or_policy_anchor: 79.8 mb Japan IEA allocation; METI also reported 254 days of public + private oil stockpiles on 2026-03-03
duration_basis: allocation divided by F6R low/base/high inventory-draw flow
assumptions: Japan has the cleanest oil bridge case: METI named replacement routes and estimated alternative procurement covered about 60% of May and 70%+ of June would-have-been Hormuz crude.
replenishment_limits: Private reserves began first, national reserves began later, and a third national release was canceled after procurement improved; actual draw is policy-gated.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Days are a residual-bridge calculation, not a claim that all 254 stockpile days can be deployed at once.

stock_or_policy_anchor: 22.5 mb Korea IEA allocation; KNOC public materials show 99.49 mb strategic reserves at end-2024 and 146 mb capacity
duration_basis: allocation divided by F6R low/base/high inventory-draw flow
assumptions: Uses IEA allocation for conservative release-duration math; total strategic stock snapshot is larger but not a public drawdown schedule.
replenishment_limits: No public weekly reserve-release series found; actual usable bridge depends on release decisions, refinery slate, and replacement crude availability.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Korea has strong exposure but weaker public evidence on actual replacement volumes and demand curtailment than Japan.

stock_or_policy_anchor: 5.33 MMT Phase-I SPR from PPAC FAQ converted at 7.33 bbl/tonne to about 39.1 mb
duration_basis: converted stock volume divided by F6R low/base/high inventory-draw flow
assumptions: India reported a rapid non-Hormuz sourcing shift, so the SPR proxy is a residual cushion rather than the main adjustment channel.
replenishment_limits: Public 2026 releases reviewed emphasize commercial/government supply management, not a quantified SPR draw; Phase-II capacity is approved in principle rather than current stock.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Conversion is approximate and crude-quality/logistics constraints matter; crude demand is less seasonal than fertilizer/gas priority allocation.

stock_or_policy_anchor: Market reporting cited about 25 mb already drawn by 2026-06-07 and about 1 mb/d expected commercial stock draw; EIA estimated much larger commercial and government-held inventories but ownership/use is opaque
duration_basis: reported 25 mb draw divided by F6R low/base/high inventory-draw flow
assumptions: This row intentionally uses the smaller reported draw, not China's broad inventory estimate, because public evidence does not isolate government SPR use.
replenishment_limits: Government SPR use is possible but unquantified; commercial stocks, bonded/floating barrels, import timing, refinery-run cuts, and product-export controls are mixed together in public data.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Do not present as government SPR duration. It is a short-run observed/reported commercial draw proxy.

stock_or_policy_anchor: 565.62 TWh EU storage at 50.03% full on 2026-07-06; equals 56.7 days of 2025 average EU inland gas demand
duration_basis: observed stock divided by average daily demand
assumptions: Europe has the cleanest public daily gas stock series, but the denominator is whole EU gas demand rather than a Hormuz-only import loss.
replenishment_limits: Storage withdrawal deliverability, injections, pipeline/LNG imports, and national interconnection bottlenecks constrain usable emergency coverage.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Average-demand days overstate winter/peak coverage and understate shoulder-season coverage.

stock_or_policy_anchor: METI reported LNG stock for about 21 days of national consumption; IEA capacity ceiling is about 36 days
duration_basis: policy-reported current stock days
assumptions: Use the 21-day current stock statement as the conservative row; 36 days is a capacity ceiling, not observed emergency stock.
replenishment_limits: No regular public LNG stock series after the survey discontinuation; cargo sharing and replacement procurement affect actual duration.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Japan's Qatar LNG exposure is modest relative to crude exposure, so whole-demand days are conservative for Qatar-only interruption but not for systemwide gas stress.

stock_or_policy_anchor: 7 days mandatory inventory plus 30 days preventive reserve; KOGAS capacity 6.56 bcm across 74 tanks
duration_basis: statutory reserve days
assumptions: This is a rule-based reserve floor, not a contemporaneous public inventory reading.
replenishment_limits: Korea has no underground gas storage; practical coverage depends on tank fill, boil-off, terminal operations, and Qatar cargo continuity.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Qatar is a major supplier, but public data do not translate the 37-day rule into observed crisis drawdown.

stock_or_policy_anchor: 22.6 bcm cumulative peak-shaving capacity against up to 425 bcm 2024 gas consumption gives 19.4 average-demand days
duration_basis: capacity proxy divided by average daily demand
assumptions: Treat as an upper-bound proxy, not a measured inventory series.
replenishment_limits: Peak-shaving capacity is not instantly drawable stock; regional pipeline/storage constraints and winter deliverability reduce usable duration.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: National average demand hides strong seasonality and regional constraints; LNG-specific exposure is not separated.

buffer_metric: No public national storage stock series
stock_or_policy_anchor: Official disruption denominator: 47.4 MMSCMD affected by force majeure and 30 MMSCMD Gulf-source gas disruption; public evidence supports rationing rather than storage days
duration_basis: qualitative / no defensible duration
assumptions: F6R estimates only low inventory draw and material demand curtailment; public stock data are insufficient for days of cover.
replenishment_limits: No GIE/KOGAS-style national storage inventory was found; emergency response relied on control orders, cargo replacement, and priority allocation.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Gas demand is priority-rationed by sector; affected-supply denominator is not whole-economy average demand.

stock_or_policy_anchor: India said about 60% of LPG consumption is imported and about 90% of imports come through Hormuz; F6R infers 0.03/0.06/0.10 mb/d inventory draw
duration_basis: qualitative / no defensible duration
assumptions: Public evidence supports control orders, domestic yield maximization, alternative suppliers, and booking/allocation restrictions, not a stockpile day count.
replenishment_limits: Usable LPG stocks depend on refinery yield, import terminals, cylinder distribution, and allocation rules; no public national stock series was found.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Demand management differs for households, commercial LPG, and petrochemical C3/C4 streams, so a single days-of-cover denominator would be misleading.

stock_or_policy_anchor: SLNG nameplate tanks imply 15.6 days of 2024 gas supply if full and fully usable
duration_basis: capacity ceiling divided by average daily gas supply
assumptions: Use only as a Southeast Asia case-study ceiling; actual inventory is lower than tank nameplate capacity.
replenishment_limits: Tank heel, trading/reload services, commercial ownership, and pipeline/LNG supply mix limit usable emergency stocks.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Annual gas denominator masks daily power-sector peaks and dual-fuel switching.

stock_or_policy_anchor: APERC/APEC exercise estimates 36 days of stage-1 Qatargas disruption coverage
duration_basis: modeled scenario-affected supply coverage
assumptions: This is a stress-test result, not a live inventory report.
replenishment_limits: Current stock, cargo replacement, and demand response may differ from the exercise assumptions.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Denominator is affected LNG supply, not total national gas demand.

buffer_metric: Seasonal closing stock divided by average seasonal sales
stock_or_policy_anchor: About 49.01 LMT closing stock on 2026-03-05 supports about 41 days of Rabi sales proxy
duration_basis: seasonal stock divided by average daily reported season-to-date sales
assumptions: India is the best public fertilizer buffer case; global fertilizer markets lack strategic reserves.
replenishment_limits: Stocks are distributed and policy-managed; imports, subsidies, allocations, and planting calendar drive usable duration.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Highly seasonal denominator; not a true daily inventory ledger and not directly a low/base/high Hormuz disruption model.

buffer_metric: Seasonal closing stock divided by average seasonal sales
stock_or_policy_anchor: About 21.61 LMT closing stock on 2026-03-05 supports about 67 days of Rabi sales proxy
duration_basis: seasonal stock divided by average daily reported season-to-date sales
assumptions: Use as a country case study, not a global phosphate inventory claim.
replenishment_limits: DAP imports and allocations can replenish or redirect supply, but China and Gulf exposure constrain replacement costs/timing.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Highly seasonal denominator; DAP demand peaks around planting and differs from average sales proxy.

buffer_metric: Seasonal closing stock divided by average seasonal sales
stock_or_policy_anchor: About 8.00 LMT closing stock on 2026-03-05 supports about 123 days of Rabi sales proxy
duration_basis: seasonal stock divided by average daily reported season-to-date sales
assumptions: MOP is more useful as a control/comparator than as a headline Hormuz-sensitive row.
replenishment_limits: Replacement depends on potash exporters outside the Gulf; Hormuz relevance is lower than urea, ammonia, sulfur, or DAP.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Highly seasonal denominator and lower direct Hormuz exposure.

buffer_metric: Seasonal closing stock divided by average seasonal sales
stock_or_policy_anchor: About 45.51 LMT closing stock on 2026-03-05 supports about 113 days of Rabi sales proxy
duration_basis: seasonal stock divided by average daily reported season-to-date sales
assumptions: Useful for domestic fertilizer availability context, but product composition is mixed.
replenishment_limits: Replenishment depends on nutrient-specific imports and domestic blending, not a single commodity stockpile.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Highly seasonal denominator; mixed NPKS product group makes direct Hormuz mapping weak.

stock_or_policy_anchor: FAO says no strategic reserves exist for fertilizers; IFA says 34% of global urea trade moves via Hormuz and 800 kt was blocked west of Hormuz at end-April
duration_basis: qualitative only
assumptions: Do not convert global urea exposure to stockpile days without country-level inventory and seasonal demand data.
replenishment_limits: Replenishment depends on alternative exporters, subsidies, planting calendar, freight, and feedstock gas/ammonia availability.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Demand destruction can mean deferred purchases or lower application, not immediate consumption cuts.

stock_or_policy_anchor: IFA/IEA rows indicate about half of seaborne sulfur trade moves via Hormuz; no public stock series supports days of cover
duration_basis: qualitative only
assumptions: Sulfur is a strong disruption story but a weak inventory-buffer story in public data.
replenishment_limits: Replenishment depends on sour-gas/oil processing, terminal stocks, and phosphate-chain operating rates.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: Inventory and demand denominators are industrial/feedstock specific, not final consumer demand.

stock_or_policy_anchor: Public evidence supports feedstock exposure and price effects, not inventory days
duration_basis: qualitative only
assumptions: Treat petrochemicals as a mechanism channel rather than a stockpile buffer row until proprietary or official product inventories are available.
replenishment_limits: Replacement is constrained by LPG/naphtha availability, cracker configurations, contract supply, and downstream demand.
seasonality_or_denominator_caveats: One denominator would obscure product-specific seasonality and industrial run-rate choices.
